title: KDNA Artifact Envelope
description: Standard envelope for any artifact produced from KDNA-governed generation. Universally references the KDNA assets, upstream artifacts, trace records, quality results, and human review status associated with a single generated artifact. Business-specific content is carried in the content field under its own schema.

artifact_id.description: Unique artifact identifier (UUID v4 or content-hash URN). Stable across reprocessing — the same logical artifact retains the same ID.

artifact_type.description: Business-level artifact type. Examples: course_package, pilot_lesson, daily_letter, assessment_report, consulting_offer. Type-specific validation uses this field to select the appropriate content schema.

schema_version.description: Semver of the artifact envelope schema this artifact conforms to.

content_schema_version.description: Semver of the business-specific content schema (e.g. the course-package schema version). Different from schema_version which tracks the envelope itself.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this artifact version was generated.

generator.description: Provenance: which engine and version produced this artifact.

engine.description: Engine identifier. Examples: course-engine, daily-letter-engine, report-engine.

version.description: Semver of the engine that generated this artifact.

run_id.description: ID of the pipeline run that produced this artifact. Links to ArtifactRun.

source_kdna.description: KDNA domains loaded during generation. Records which judgment assets governed this artifact's creation.

digest.description: Asset digest of the .kdna file loaded (sha256:hex).

role.description: Role this domain played in the generation stage.

source_artifacts.description: Upstream artifacts that served as input to this artifact. Empty for initial stages that consume raw inputs.

relationship.description: How this upstream artifact was used in generating the current artifact.

stage.description: Which pipeline stage produced this artifact. Present when the generation occurred within a multi-stage pipeline; omitted for single-step generation.

stage_attempt.description: Which attempt of this stage produced this version (1-based).

stage_status.description: Status of this stage when the artifact was recorded.

content.description: Business-specific artifact content. Must conform to the schema identified by artifact_type and content_schema_version. This field carries the actual work product — course package, daily letter, assessment report, etc.

content_digest.description: SHA-256 digest of the canonical JSON serialization of content. Used for integrity verification and deduplication across reprocessing.

quality.description: Quality gate results and fidelity measurement for this artifact.

gate_results.description: Results from each quality gate applied to this artifact.

overall_result.description: Composed result across all quality gates.

fidelity.description: Judgment transfer fidelity measurement. Computed by the Fidelity Protocol when enabled.

score.description: Overall fidelity score. 0 = no judgment transferred, 1 = complete judgment transfer.

protocol_version.description: Version of the Fidelity Protocol used for measurement.

report_artifact_id.description: Artifact ID of the full Fidelity Report, if generated as a separate artifact.

trace_refs.description: Judgment trace identifiers produced during this artifact's generation. Links to the KDNA trace system.

trace_type.description: Which phase of the pipeline produced this trace.

review.description: Human review status. Null before review; populated after a human reviews and approves/rejects.

review_notes.description: Reviewer's notes or rationale for the decision.

metadata.description: Extensible metadata. Implementations may add namespaced fields.
